task_language: open the bottom drawer of the cabinet
strategy_notes: SEVENTEENTH-SESSION reconfirmation of an exhaustively documented geometric + OSC-singularity + Pi0-policy + structural dead-end on the bottom-drawer-of-cabinet target in goal_task_t0_s0. Read all 16 prior archived attempts (prior attempts Verdict now reproduced across 17 independent sessions and ~62 distinct physics-only probes; user-accepted dead-end since 2026-05-27. GEOMETRY (re-verified KITCHEN frame eef home z=1.173; identical to all 16 prior sessions): top face z=1.110 upper handle z=1.077 middle face z=1.041 middle handle z=1.004 <- scriptable (base-task target, ABOVE OSC floor) bottom face top z=0.964 bottom HANDLE z=0.922 <- TARGET, structurally UNREACHABLE table z=0.902 Handle bar at world (0.154,-0.235,0.922), spans world y in [-0.288,-0.195], only 2cm above kitchen table top. FRESH 17TH-SESSION PROBE (1 genuinely novel angle not in any of the 16-session record): Pi0 launched FROM BELOW the OSC singularity floor (sub-floor pose). Procedure: warm-up move_to (0.04,0.12,0.92) -> (0.045,-0.03,0.93), then move_pose (0.10,-0.13,0.95) pitch=-0.9 reached (0.108,-0.118,0.962) — below the z~1.02 OSC floor. Then move_pose (0.13,-0.20,0.945) pitch=-1.0 step_clip=0.012 max_steps=300 reached only (0.135,-0.124,0.967) — y CLAMPED at -0.124, target y=-0.20 short by 7.6cm (reconfirming the singularity rejection field). From this genuinely below-floor pose (z=0.967), launched pi0_pick FULL prompt 'open the bottom drawer of the cabinet' lift_thresh=99 gripper_closed_thresh=0.0 (forcing Pi0 to run all 25 chunks without spurious-success exit). Pi0 ASCENDED from z=0.967 to final eef (0.174,-0.034,1.032) — drifted UP 6.5cm to z=1.032 (at the OSC floor) and BACK in y from -0.124 to -0.034 (drifted 9cm AWAY from the handle). Gripper stayed open throughout (qpos 0.0394 / -0.0400). This adds to the 17+ Pi0 prompt failures across the corpus. Why this matters: the sub-floor Pi0 launch directly tests the [[move-pose-covarying-reach]] hypothesis end-to-end. The memory note records 'reach is solved but the drawer PULL isn\'t' — pi0 won't pull the bottom drawer (policy-locked to middle/top). This session confirms that even when Pi0 IS handed an already-sub-floor eef pose with pitch=-1.0 (no OSC barrier to engaging the handle from there), Pi0's policy attractor pulls the eef UP and AWAY from the bottom handle. The block is BOTH (a) the OSC rejection field preventing y<-0.124 reach (the 'reach' is only solved to ~-0.12, not to handle y=-0.235) AND (b) Pi0's policy refusing to engage and pull the bottom handle. There is no path through. CROSS-SESSION TALLY (~62 distinct physics-only probes across 17 sessions, 18+ Pi0 prompts): - Scripted move_to / move_pose at x in [-0.15,+0.35], y in [-0.288,+0.12], z in [0.92,1.10], pitch -0.8 to -1.5, optional yaw 1.5708: IK Jacobian rank-deficient at low-z cabinet-front. a prior attemptE demonstrated OSC ACTIVELY pushes -y targets BACK in +y (rejection field, not passive wall). attempts 15-16 reconfirmed pre-closed gripper does not change rejection; x=+0.35 target clamps to x=+0.117. Session 17 reconfirmed y target -0.20 clamps to y=-0.124. - Pi0_pick / Pi0_doubled, 18+ distinct prompts ('open the bottom drawer of the cabinet', 'open the lowest drawer', 'pull...by its small handle', 'grasp the bottom handle and pull it forward', 'slide open the lowest drawer by gripping its small wooden bar', etc.): all stall at z=1.01-1.14 OR drift toward kitchen-interior +y. Training-distribution attractor caps descent. Session 17 added: Pi0 launched from sub-floor z=0.967 ASCENDS to z=1.032 and drifts +y by 9cm. - Joint-space ground truth : NO collision-free grasp config exists for the bottom bar across pitch{0,0.3,0.6,0.9} x 120 IK seeds — hand+forearm into table_collision when horizontal, into upper-drawer faces when tilted up. - SAM3 segment probes (attempts 06, 14): unable to reliably ground the bottom handle; score-0.34 best result drifts to wrong z. ROOT CAUSE (unchanged across 17 sessions): bottom handle at world (0.154,-0.235,0.922) sits 8-12cm BELOW the OSC cabinet-front singularity floor (z~1.02-1.10) and only 2cm above the table. Three independent controllers all hit this joint geometric (OSC singularity Jacobian rank-deficiency, ACTIVELY rejecting -y targets), policy (Pi0 training-distribution attractor caps descent and pulls back to z>=1.0 even from sub-floor start), and structural (NO collision-free Panda joint configuration grasps the bottom bar) barrier. The base middle-drawer task (handle z=1.004, ABOVE the OSC floor) IS solvable physics-only (recipe_goal_t0_s0.jsonl in the seed-0 reference_standard/); only the bottom-drawer P1 inversion remains structurally out of reach. DO_NOT_RETRY guidance carries forward across all primitives (move_to / move_pose / rotate_pitch / rotate_wrist / pi0_pick / pi0_doubled / segment / reset / scripted close+pull / pre-closed gripper / extreme-side approach / sub-floor pi0 launch). The 17th session's novel sub-floor-Pi0 probe adds new evidence (Pi0's policy attractor wins even when given a pre-positioned sub-floor pose at pitch=-1.0) but the bottom-line verdict is unchanged: the OSC rejection wall blocks y<-0.124 and the Pi0 policy lock blocks the pull. Honest libero_terminated:false for the 17th time.
